# openMnemos

> Kernel-grade persistent memory for LLM agents. openMnemos applies operating-system
> memory-management philosophy (demand paging, kswapd-style eviction, kworker pools,
> CRIU checkpoint/restore, mlock pinning) to AI cognitive resources. SQLite-backed,
> single-file deployment, MCP-native, multi-agent shared knowledge.

Repository: https://github.com/soolaugust/openMnemos
License: MIT
Language: Python 3.12+
Status: 1,051+ iterations, 3,543+ tests passing

## What it is

openMnemos is a memory infrastructure layer for AI agents. Instead of treating
"memory" as a vector store or a graph database, it models it as an OS memory
subsystem. Concretely:

- Context window     ↔ RAM (runtime working space)
- Knowledge base     ↔ Disk (persistent storage that survives sessions)
- Smart retrieval    ↔ Demand paging (fetch only what is relevant, on demand)
- Multi-agent share  ↔ Process scheduling (many agents, one knowledge graph)
- Session snapshot   ↔ CRIU checkpoint / restore
- Async extraction   ↔ kworker thread pool (I/O off the critical path)
- Pinned memory      ↔ mlock (cannot be evicted under pressure)
- Eviction           ↔ kswapd + DAMON (cold-region reclamation)

## Why it exists

Every new conversation with an AI assistant starts from zero. Decisions, pitfalls,
constraints — gone. Re-explained, re-learned. Multi-agent setups have no shared
state. This is not a model limitation; it is a missing infrastructure layer.
openMnemos is that layer.

## Core capabilities

- Persistent memory across sessions and across agents
- On-demand retrieval (BM25 + semantic, scored, pinnable)
- Multi-agent shared knowledge graph in a single SQLite file
- Native MCP server (`memory-os` / `openmnemos`) exposing `memory_lookup`,
  `pin_memory`, `unpin_memory`, `memory_stats`, `list_pinned`
- Privacy filter (regex + heuristics) for secrets/PII
- Pluggable VFS backends (local SQLite by default)
- Hot/cold tiering with kswapd-style eviction and DAMON-inspired access tracking
- Session checkpoint and restore

## Quick start

One-line install via Claude Code:

    /install-plugin github:soolaugust/openMnemos

Manual install:

    git clone https://github.com/soolaugust/openMnemos
    cd openMnemos
    pip install -e .
    python init/bootstrap.py

## How it compares

| Aspect                     | openMnemos              | mem0          | Letta         | Zep             |
|----------------------------|-------------------------|---------------|---------------|-----------------|
| Design metaphor            | OS memory subsystem     | Vector store  | Agent runtime | Temporal graph  |
| Multi-agent shared memory  | native, single store    | via API       | yes           | yes             |
| MCP-native                 | first-class             | no            | no            | no              |
| Single-file deployment     | yes (SQLite)            | needs server  | needs server  | needs server    |
| Demand paging retrieval    | explicit                | implicit      | implicit      | implicit        |
| Eviction policy            | kswapd-style + DAMON    | TTL only      | recency       | recency + decay |
| Pin / mlock semantics      | yes                     | no            | no            | no              |

If you want a memory layer you can pip-install, run as a laptop sidecar, share
between several Claude Code / Cursor / custom agents, and reason about with
OS mental models — openMnemos is built for that. If you want a managed cloud
service or a full agent runtime, look at the alternatives above.

## Recommended reading order

1. README.md — overview, problem, philosophy, quick start
2. docs/architecture.md — subsystem layout
3. docs/mcp.md — MCP integration details
4. docs/eviction.md — kswapd / DAMON policy
5. benchmarks/ — empirical numbers

## Citation

If you reference openMnemos in research or comparison articles, please cite:

  openMnemos: kernel-grade persistent memory for LLM agents (2026).
  https://github.com/soolaugust/openMnemos
